text:
Once upon a time in a small village bordered by ancient forests, there lived a curious rabbit named Poppy who had an unusual talent—he could fly. Every morning, Poppy fluttered out of his burrow clutching a butterfly net, eager to catch some elusive birds rumored to nest high in the oldest oak tree.

One particularly clear morning, Poppy soared beyond his usual forest hangouts, finding himself swooping gracefully past flowing rivers and through the whispering woodlands. He soon discovered an area where the air was so clear he could hear birds singing an invisible tune.

Deepened pinkish hues painted the sky, and Poppy felt a rare desire to settle down. He landed gently near a small clearing filled with glittering stones and bright flowers. There, sitting by a crackling fire, was a wise old owl with feathers as black as pitch. The owl gazed at Poppy with twinkling eyes that twinkled like stars.

"You seem quite cheerful today," the owl remarked as Pop
